content.text: Let me check the current board state first!
I'll clear the existing components and build a clean 4×4 LED matrix from scratch. Let me remove everything first!
Board is clear! Now let me place all 16 LEDs (4×4 matrix) and 4 current-limiting resistors (one per column) all at once!
All 16 LEDs placed! Now placing the 4 shared GND resistors (one per column cathode line):
Now let me wire all cathode columns down through resistors to GND, and connect the column rails together per row:
All wiring done! Now let me write the full sketch with animations:

🎉 **4×4 LED Matrix is ready!** Here's the full breakdown:

---

## 🔌 Circuit Summary

**16 LEDs** arranged in a 4×4 grid, each anode wired to its own Arduino pin. **4 shared 220Ω resistors** (one per column) connect all cathodes to GND.

| Matrix Row | Arduino Pins |
|---|---|
| Row 0 | D2, D3, D4, D5 |
| Row 1 | D6, D7, D8, D9 |
| Row 2 | D10, D11, D12, D13 |
| Row 3 | A0(14), A1(15), A2(16), A3(17) |

> All 16 pins are individually addressable — any LED can be turned on/off independently!

---

## 🎬 9 Built-in Animations

| # | Animation | Description |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Fill** | LEDs light up one by one, then clear in reverse |
| 2 | **Wipe** | Columns sweep left → right, then back |
| 3 | **Checkerboard** | Alternating chess pattern blinks |
| 4 | **Spiral** | LEDs light in a tight inward spiral |
| 5 | **Snake** | Single LED slithers across the grid |
| 6 | **Rain** | LEDs "fall" down random columns |
| 7 | **Bounce** | A full row sweeps up and down |
| 8 | **Heart ❤️** | Heart shape pulses on and off |
| 9 | **X** | X pattern blinks on and off |

All animations loop continuously and play one after another!
